creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Google’s Genie world model can now simulate real streets with Street View
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Google DeepMind is integrating Street View with Project Genie to create immersive, interactive world simulations for robotics, gaming, and travel, allowing users to explore environments, weather changes, and rare scenarios.
contentSnippet: Google DeepMind is integrating Street View with Project Genie to create immersive, interactive world simulations for robotics, gaming, and travel, allowing users to explore environments, weather changes, and rare scenarios.

creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) With Gemini 3.5 Flash, Google bets its next AI wave on agents, not chatbots
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash, its most powerful coding and agentic AI model yet, at the company's annual developer conference. It is capable of autonomously executing complex tasks and building software from scratch.
contentSnippet: Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash, its most powerful coding and agentic AI model yet, at the company's annual developer conference. It is capable of autonomously executing complex tasks and building software from scratch.

title: (No AI) Gemini will use Volvo’s external cameras to interpret parking signs
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![Volvo EX60](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/Volvo-EX60-Oculus-6.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Gemini is gaining the power of sight _and_ mobility. [Today at the I/O conference](https://www.theverge.com/tech/932454/google-io-2026-news-announcements), Google and Volvo announced that the AI-powered assistant will be able to access external cameras in [the upcoming EX60 SUV](https://www.theverge.com/news/864561/volvo-ex60-range-price-specs) to help explain and interpret its surroundings to vehicle owners. The upgrade is possible thanks to Volvo's use of Google's embedded Android Automotive as its vehicle operating system.

Google posits that the first use case will be to ask Gemini to translate difficult-to-understand parking signs, though the company obviously sees other future applications as possible as well. Google envisions a camera-enabled Gemini recalling a road sign, inte …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/933556/google-io-gemini-volvo-ex60-camera-ai-parking)

Gemini is gaining the power of sight and mobility. The upgrade is possible thanks to Volvo's use of Google's embedded Android Automotive as its vehicle operating system.
Google posits that the first use case will be to ask Gemini to translate difficult-to-understand parking signs, though the company obviously sees other future applications as possible as well. Google envisions a camera-enabled Gemini recalling a road sign, inte …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) The 13 biggest announcements at Google I/O 2026
author: Andrew Liszewski
content:
YouTube" data-portal-copyright="Screenshot: [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wYSncx9zLIU)" data-has-syndication-rights="1" src="https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/io2026.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100" />

Google CEO Sundar Pichai on stage at I/O 2026. | Screenshot: [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wYSncx9zLIU)

Google's I/O 2026 keynote today was once again full of AI-related announcements including a new family of Gemini 3.5 AI models, new features for Search and Gmail, and updates about its Project Aura smart glasses.

If you weren't able to [tune into the event's livestream today](https://www.theverge.com/tech/932939/google-io-2026-how-to-watch) or [follow along with our live blog](https://www.theverge.com/tech/932275/google-io-2026-live-blog-on-the-ground-at-googles-keynote), you can catch up on everything you missed in our roundup below.

## [**Gemini 3.5**](https://www.theverge.com/tech/933699/google-gemini-redesign-ai-3-5-flash-io-2026)

Google launched updated AI models at I/O, starting with Gemini 3.5 Flash, with Gemini 3.5 Pro following next month. Starting today, Gemini 3.5 Flash will be the default model for the Gemini app and AI Mode in Search. Google says the new model is significantly …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/933415/google-io-2026-biggest-announcements-ai-gemini)

title: (No AI) Advancing content provenance for a safer, more transparent AI ecosystem
content: OpenAI advances AI content provenance with Content Credentials, SynthID, and a verification tool to help people identify and trust AI-generated media.
contentSnippet: OpenAI advances AI content provenance with Content Credentials, SynthID, and a verification tool to help people identify and trust AI-generated media.

title: (No AI) OpenAI and Dell partner to bring Codex to hybrid and on-premise enterprise environments
content: OpenAI and Dell partner to bring Codex to hybrid and on-premise environments, helping enterprises deploy AI coding agents securely across data and workflows.
contentSnippet: OpenAI and Dell partner to bring Codex to hybrid and on-premise environments, helping enterprises deploy AI coding agents securely across data and workflows.

title: (No AI) Fast-tracking genetic leads to reverse cellular aging
content: Biologists use Co-Scientist to find novel factors that successfully rejuvenate human cells.
contentSnippet: Biologists use Co-Scientist to find novel factors that successfully rejuvenate human cells.

title: (No AI) Simulate real-world places with Project Genie and Street View
content: We’re expanding access to Google AI Ultra subscribers globally and introducing a new capability powered by Street View.
contentSnippet: We’re expanding access to Google AI Ultra subscribers globally and introducing a new capability powered by Street View.

creator: Will Knight
title: (No AI) Demis Hassabis Thinks AI Job Cuts Are Dumb
dc:creator: Will Knight
content: The CEO of Google DeepMind tells WIRED that companies should use the productivity gains of AI to do more, not lay people off.
contentSnippet: The CEO of Google DeepMind tells WIRED that companies should use the productivity gains of AI to do more, not lay people off.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Google Makes It Easy to Deepfake Yourself
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: Google’s overhaul of its AI creation software, Flow, includes a new video model and a tool for generating selfie videos called avatars.
contentSnippet: Google’s overhaul of its AI creation software, Flow, includes a new video model and a tool for generating selfie videos called avatars.
